summary: Accepted SIM-001 architecture re-review for WorldState v0 after REQUEST_CHANGES fixes and lead integration supplements. Current worktree satisfies branded ID boundaries, Definition/Runtime State separation, minimal M1 WorldState scope, canonical hash stability, structured invariant validation, runtime table exact coverage, meta schema validation, and strengthened sim-core architecture guard coverage.

tests_run.result: Vitest ran tests/sim-core-world-state.test.ts: 1 file passed, 10 tests passed.
Architecture boundaries passed. Self-test fixture produced 15 expected violation(s).
architecture:check passed; sim determinism matched Node and Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; root Vitest 2 files/12 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

decisions: WorldState v0 remains within SIM-001 M1 kernel scope and does not introduce economy, population, diplomacy, war, save envelope, historical DB, runtime DB, server, Rust/WASM, or new production dependency.
Malformed external world inputs are treated as schema/invariant errors instead of being allowed to throw during validation.
Architecture review accepts route to independent QA final acceptance before task closure.
risks: Current branch contains uncommitted implementation and orchestration files; final QA should review the same worktree state or a committed equivalent.
requested_action: QA reviewer: perform independent final acceptance for SIM-001 using the current worktree or committed equivalent, including the documented commands and scope checks.
